summary: Stancil argues that the modern data stack boom was driven as much by hype and attention-seeking as by genuine technical conviction, and that the post-ChatGPT shift to AI has hollowed out that “club.” The slowdown creates a better moment for durable startups to rebuild proven ideas more deliberately, while he also critiques the industry’s near-religious faith that “data” is inherently valuable.
money_quotes: Hype cycles are great for building, but not great for enduring.
They just have to rebuild what’s already there, but more deliberately and on more certain ground than the original pioneers could.
This sort of message has become so ubiquitous that it’s become our water.
Wherever there’s data, there’s power—is the very thing that makes people believe.
key_insight: The data-stack hype cycle is fading, and the real opportunity now is in rebuilding established ideas thoughtfully while questioning the industry’s faith that data is inherently valuable.
